data: [{"name":"github__list_pull_requests","description":"List pull requests, optionally filtered by repo, state (open/merged/closed), or author.","signature":"github__list_pull_requests({repo?: string, state?: string, author?: string}) → { number: number, repo: \"acme/infra\"|\"acme/web\"|\"acme/api\"|\…(+1537)

[{"name":"linear__list_issues","description":"List Linear issues, optionally filtered by assignee, state, team, or project.","signature":"linear__list_issues({assignee?: string, state?: string, team?: string, project?: string}) → { id: string, title: string, assignee: string, state: \"in_progress\"|…(+1028)

tool_calls.name: execute_python
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
prs = github.list_pull_requests(state="merged")
# PR rows have a `closes_linear` field — let's see
[(p["number"], p["closes_linear"]) for p in prs]
"}

tool_calls.name: execute_python
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
closes = [(p["number"], p["closes_linear"]) for p in prs if p["closes_linear"]]
# Fetch each linked Linear issue
results = []
for n, lid in closes:
    iss = linear.get_issue(id=lid)
    results.append((n, lid, iss["state"]))
results
"}

tool_calls.name: execute_python
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
non_done = [(n, lid, st) for n, lid, st in results if st != "done"]
non_done, len(non_done)
"}
